three distinct layers over time:

Immediate / Technical Layer (Execution Time):
Example: "Did the push_fix API return a 200 OK?" (Logged instantly at the gateway).
Short-Term / Session Layer (Minutes to Hours):
Example: "Did the CI/CD build actually pass 30 minutes later? Did the customer reply to the support ticket angrily within 2 hours?"
Long-Term / Business Layer (Days to Months):
Example: "Did that fix hold up for the week? Did that customer churn 30 days later?"

Fast Mode: Skip Eyes on high-confidence pages. Cache decompositions. Pre-warm Chrome.

  Normal:  Decompose(10s) → Plan(10s) → Execute(5s) → 5 Eyes(20s) → Verify(10s) = 55s
  Fast:    Cache hit(0s) → Execute(5s)                         = 5s

  Implementation: LanceDB already has scored actions. Extend it to cache goal_hash → sub_goals + plan. On cache hit, entire pipeline is DOM execute only. On miss, full pipeline runs once, caches result.
